                  1. Home
                  2. Services
                  3. Prescription Drug Coverage

                  CVS Caremark® pharmacy benefit manager

                  As the leading pharmacy benefits manager in the U.S., CVS Caremark, part of CVS Health®, collaborates with employers, unions and health plans to expand coverage to affordable medications, deliver better health outcomes and help lower overall health care costs for millions of people across the country.

                  The impact of pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs)

                  PBMs support the health care system by helping clients and members manage prescription drug costs, improve medication adherence and promote clinically appropriate use of prescription drugs. Through formulary strategies, clinical programs and negotiated discounts, PBMs work to improve affordability and access to prescription drugs.

                  • CVS Caremark facts at a glance (PDF)
                  • 5 facts to know about PBMs (PDF)

                  What is CVS Caremark?

                  CVS Caremark, part of CVS Health, plays a critical role as a pharmacy benefit manager in the health care system by negotiating low net costs for our members while supporting safe and clinically effective access to medications. Beyond traditional PBMs, CVS Caremark uses an integrated model to increase access to care, improve health outcomes and help lower overall health care costs.

                  CVS Caremark takes a customized approach to meet the unique needs of each member, prioritizing transparency into how health care dollars are spent and leading with clinical integrity to support safe and clinically-effective products for the people it serves.

                  Discover the Caremark difference

                  Get to know pharmacy benefit managers

                  Pharmacy benefit managers, or PBMs, manage prescription drug benefits for clients ranging from health insurers and Medicare Part D drug plans to large employers. PBMs are one of the few parts of the prescription drug supply chain specifically dedicated to lowering costs.

                   

                  People are more likely to take their prescribed medications when they know they can afford them. Across every therapeutic category, we strive to achieve low costs for our clients and their members. That means managing the two fundamental forces behind drug spending: price and utilization.

                  CVS Caremark negotiates lower costs for our clients and expands coverage to affordable medications that people need to stay healthy. We are transparent about medication costs and prioritize a high-quality and more affordable approach to health care.

                  We use utilization management (UM) to help support clinically appropriate coverage of high-cost medications and keep costs in check.1

                  As one example of how we help lower out-of-pocket prescription costs, our members may use our proprietary online search tool for savings options that work with their prescription plan.

                  Find ways CVS Caremark® helps keep drug costs down

                  The basic services PBMs provide include:

                  • Negotiating discounts off prices set by drug manufacturers
                  • Creating and administering retail pharmacy networks
                  • Helping members understand the best use of pharmacy benefits
                  • Developing and maintaining formularies, which are lists of covered drugs
                  • Providing mail pharmacy and home delivery services
                  • Processing pharmacy claims
                  Explore our mail service pharmacy

                  CVS Caremark goes beyond the basic services of a PBM by providing adherence unseling to make sure members take medications as prescribed and help close gaps in care. We also support members with chronic and specialty conditions, provide disease management support and more.

                  All in all, the goal of these added services is to help improve health outcomes, which helps lower long-term and overall health care costs. Almost half of our members use our digital tools to manage their prescriptions, find lower-cost alternatives and stay on track with their treatments.

                  Discover our approach to care management

                  With traditional medical health insurance, medical bills are covered by insurance benefits for hospitals, doctors and other health care provider costs. Traditional medical health insurance also covers medications administered directly by the provider in the office or at the hospital.

                  The prescription drug benefit covers medications that are typically picked up at the pharmacy or delivered to the home.

                  PBMs work alongside health plans and employers to administer prescription drug benefits. Depending on a person’s insurance, they may be able to use their health insurance card when they pick up prescriptions at the pharmacy, or they may have a separate card specifically for prescription drug coverage.

                  CVS Caremark members have the option to fill their prescriptions at a broad network of over 64,000 participating pharmacies, including CVS Pharmacy locations.2 Pharmacy options may vary by plan. Members can view their specific network by signing in to their account and using the pharmacy locator tool.3

                  For years, the pharmacy industry managed drug costs through market basket pricing — prices based on a basket of various medications, aiming to lower the cost of brand drugs by shifting value from generic drugs.

                  TrueCost, the pricing model by CVS Caremark, reduces pricing disparities and helps members realize the maximum value of our purchasing power so they can take full advantage of their prescription drug benefits. When they stay on benefit, members get a connected experience with digital tools and clinical support, helping to improve their health outcomes.

                  How we're reshaping the future of drug pricing

                  • 1https://business.caremark.com/what-we-do/cost-management/utilization-management.html

                  • 2https://business.caremark.com/insights/2024/supporting-local-access-community-pharmacies.html

                  • 3https://www.caremark.com/pharmacy/benefits/public/help-center
